title: Ordered-cat plus within-lap rank ensemble with sigmoid-only calibration
summary: CatBoost’s ordered categorical handling is explicitly designed to reduce target leakage from categorical statistics, and XGBoost ranking supports grouped query learning.
rationale: CatBoost’s ordered categorical handling is explicitly designed to reduce target leakage from categorical statistics, and XGBoost ranking supports grouped query learning. For this task, a ranker can answer 'who is most likely to pit on this lap?' while a classifier answers absolute probability. Blend them, then use only rank-preserving sigmoid calibration because isotonic can alter ROC-AUC via ties.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoost classifier on raw categorical columns and combinations instead of manual frequency encodings, then train an XGBoost ranker with qid = Year/Race/LapNumber and binary label = PitNextLap. Blend only if both improve on leave-race-out plus rolling-year CV. If calibration helps, apply OOF sigmoid/Platt scaling to the blend, not isotonic.
expected_effect: Adds model diversity and may improve lap-local ordering without sacrificing absolute probability quality; often useful when baseline tree models are already saturated.
risk: Ranking groups with very few positives can be unstable, and CatBoost/ranker gains may disappear if validation is not truly race-held-out. Isotonic calibration is a trap here because it can change AUC.
